Name: curvature_aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient Mixed-Precision Quantization
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that a curvature-aware precision routing framework can efficiently optimize mixed-precision quantization in deep neural networks, leading to improved accuracy and reduced computational costs. By integrating spectral quantization theory with low-overhead implementation, we can develop a framework that bridges foundational optimization theory with deployment constraints.
Related Work: Recent works such as EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT have explored mixed-precision quantization and training-free proxies. However, our proposal distinguishes itself by focusing on curvature-aware precision routing, which has not been extensively explored in the context of mixed-precision quantization. Our approach builds upon the ideas presented in Mix-GEMM and HiLoRA, but with a unique focus on curvature-aware routing.
Abstract: This proposal introduces a novel curvature-aware precision routing framework for efficient mixed-precision quantization in deep neural networks. By leveraging spectral quantization theory and low-overhead implementation, our framework optimizes precision routing to minimize accuracy loss while reducing computational costs. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through extensive experiments on various benchmark datasets, showcasing improved accuracy and reduced computational costs compared to state-of-the-art baselines.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Evaluate the effectiveness of our curvature-aware precision routing framework on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet datasets using ResNet and MobileNet architectures.
Experiment 2: Compare the performance of our framework with state-of-the-art mixed-precision quantization methods, including EMQ and SNIP.
Experiment 3: Investigate the impact of curvature-aware routing on the accuracy and computational costs of vision transformers (ViTs) using the AMP-ViT framework.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of the curvature-aware routing algorithm may lead to increased computational overhead.
The effectiveness of our framework may depend on the specific dataset and architecture used.
The lack of theoretical guarantees for the optimality of the curvature-aware routing framework may limit its applicability.
